good.text: They won't use your name or likeness in marketing without consent.
Children's data gets extra protection or isn't collected.
They extend GDPR/CCPA-style rights to all users.
You keep full ownership of the content you create.
They promise advance notice before terms change.

bad.text: You give up your right to sue in court over disputes.
They can change the terms at any time without telling you.
Your content and data are used to train their AI models.
They take a broad, perpetual, sublicensable license to your content.
They can suspend or close your account at any time, for any reason.
They fingerprint your device to track you without cookies.
You must cover their legal costs if claims arise from your use.
Their liability to you is capped at almost nothing.
Your content or balance can be erased when an account ends.
Any lawsuit must happen in their distant home court.
All payments are non-refundable, even unused time.
They can raise prices at any time, sometimes without notice.
Subscriptions auto-renew and keep charging you.
The service is provided 'as is' with no guarantees.

By submitting Inputs to our Services, you represent and warrant that you have all rights, licenses, and permissions that are necessary for us to process the Inputs under our Terms and to provide the Services to you, including for example, to integrate with third-party services, to share Materials with others at your direction, and to take Actions. clauses.label: Advance Notice of Changes
clauses.summary: The company may modify the terms, but any changes become effective only after a 30‑day notice period, giving the customer advance warning.
clauses.excerpt: M.3. Amendment and Modification. Anthropic may update these Terms at any time, to be effective 30 days after the updates are posted by Anthropic or Customer otherwise receives Notice, except that updates made in response to changes to law or regulation take effect immediately upon posting or Notice. Changes will not apply retroactively. No other amendment to or modification of these Terms is effective unless it is in writing and signed by both parties. Failure to exercise or delay in exercising any rights or remedies arising from these Terms does not and will not be construed as a waiver; and

clauses.label: No AI Training / Opt-Out
clauses.summary: The policy says your inputs and outputs may be used to train models but lets you opt out in account settings, though some flagged data may still be used.

clauses.label: No AI Training / Opt-Out
clauses.summary: States that the customer retains all rights to its inputs and outputs and that Anthropic will not use the customer's content to train its models.
clauses.excerpt:
B. Customer Content
As between the parties and to the extent permitted by applicable law, Anthropic agrees that Customer (a) retains all rights to its Inputs, and (b) owns its Outputs. Anthropic disclaims any rights it receives to the Customer Content under these Terms. Subject to Customer’s compliance with these Terms, Anthropic hereby assigns to Customer its right, title and interest (if any) in and to Outputs. Anthropic may not train models on Customer Content from Services. “Inputs” means submissions to the Services by Customer or its Users and “Outputs” means responses generated by the Se

history.summary: Several changes to the Consumer Terms of Service allow Anthropic to modify user agreements and liability. The company can now require additional terms that alter user rights or obligations and link accounts to enterprise accounts with monitoring. Users' ability to get refunds for payments has been restricted. The company also reserves the right to change, suspend, or end services at any time without liability for those actions. Furthermore, Anthropic has broadened its limitations on liability and damages users can claim. No protective changes were noted.

The company can limit, pause, or end your access if you don't follow the Usage Policy. This means your service could be interrupted or stopped if you violate the rules. No friendly provisions were found in this review.

Changes to the Commercial Terms of Service allow Anthropic to use customer feedback without obligation, but also protect customers by stating they retain rights to inputs and outputs and can opt out of name and logo use. However, customers may face limitations, including broad company control over account termination, forced arbitration, and limited liability for Anthropic. The company disclaims warranties and limits liability to fees paid in the prior year. No major user-friendly changes were found.

The company can now change the Responsible Disclosure Policy at any time by posting a new version, without giving users any notice. This means users will have to keep checking for updates and will be bound by the new terms immediately. No protections for users were found that would counterbalance this change.
No changes were found that negatively impact users. The review did not reveal any user-hostile updates to the Cookie Policy document.

No changes to report, the policy appears to be protecting users' rights to privacy and data protection, no user-hostile changes were found. The policy still requires the company to obtain consent before collecting and sharing consumer health data. The company is also still obligated to provide users with control over their data and to keep it confidential. Overall, no significant changes were identified that would negatively impact users.

The company updated its privacy policy to collect more device and connection data to track users across services. The policy also allows the company to use user data, including inputs and outputs, to train its AI models, although users can opt out in account settings. Additionally, the policy informs users of their legal data-protection rights and outlines the process for exercising those rights. No major changes were found that would indicate a significant shift against users' interests beyond existing data collection and AI training practices.
